channel.title: Tech
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Tech, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Google launches Nano Banana 2 model with faster image generation
item.description: Google is making Nano Banana 2 a default model in Gemini app and in AI mode

item.title: Instagram now alerts parents if their teen searches for suicide or self-harm content
item.description: Parents will be informed if their teen searches for suicide or self-harm content and offered resources.

item.title: Cisco says hackers have been exploiting a critical bug to break into big customer networks since 2023
item.description: The U.S. government and its allies said hackers have been exploiting the newly identified bug in Cisco networking gear around the world for years, and...

item.title: Self-driving truck startup Einride raises $113M PIPE ahead of public debut
item.description: Self-driving trucks startup Einride has raised an oversubscribed $113 million PIPE in advance of its SPAC merger scheduled for early 2026.

item.title: How to avoid bad hires in early-stage startups
item.description: Lucena got the idea for Mappa after trying to build a marketing team but continually feeling like she had made the wrong hires.

item.title: Best Tested Ski Clothes (2026): Shells, Jackets, Wool Socks
item.description: From weatherproof jackets and pants to puffers, gloves, and socks, WIRED’s winter sports experts have got you covered.

item.title: Who’s Your Daddy? A Chatbot
item.description: More people are turning to AI to explore their BDSM fantasies, but some in the community feel it’s a cop-out.

item.title: Everything Samsung revealed at its Galaxy Unpacked event
item.description: Samsung's new privacy screen feature on Galaxy S26 Ultra was the most notable feature of the event.

item.title: Spyware maker sentenced to prison in Greece for wiretapping politicians and journalists
item.description: A Greek court on Thursday sentenced the founder of Intellexa, a collective of spyware makers, to eight years in prison for illegal wiretapping and pri...

item.title: A VC and some big-name programmers are trying to solve open source’s funding problem, permanently
item.description: A group of well-known open source programmers and a VC have launched the Open Source Endowment. They hope this new method will provide funding for goo...
